title: Mr. Kumar's NYC Years
description: USED TO and WOULD for past habits and repeated actions. Mr. Kumar tells how he ran a bookstore in New York — and what he used to do every rainy Monday.
summary_pt_br: USED TO e WOULD para hábitos passados. Diferença: USED TO serve para estados e ações ('I used to live in NY'), WOULD só para ações repetidas ('I would walk to the café every morning'). Com states (live, be, have), NUNCA use WOULD.

scenes.subtitle_en: What I used to do in another life
scenes.subtitle_pt: O que eu costumava fazer noutra vida

scenes.text_en:
'I USED TO own a bookstore in New York,' Mr. Kumar said.
'On rainy Mondays, I WOULD open late. I WOULD make a pot of chai.
Customers WOULD wander in, shake their umbrellas, ask for novels.
I USED TO know every title on every shelf. Not anymore.'
scenes.text_pt:
'Eu COSTUMAVA ter uma livraria em Nova York,' disse Mr. Kumar.
'Nas segundas chuvosas, eu ABRIA tarde. PREPARAVA um bule de chai.
Os clientes ENTRAVAM, sacudiam o guarda-chuva, pediam romances.
Eu CONHECIA cada título em cada prateleira. Não mais.'

scenes.title: USED TO vs WOULD — the subtle split
scenes.body_en: USED TO works for BOTH past states ('I used to live in NY', 'she used to be shy') AND past repeated actions ('we used to meet every Sunday'). WOULD works ONLY for past repeated actions ('we would meet every Sunday'). Never use WOULD with state verbs — 'I would live in NY' doesn't mean habit; it means hypothetical (Second Conditional!).
scenes.body_pt: USED TO = passado para ESTADOS + AÇÕES repetidas. WOULD = só AÇÕES repetidas. Nunca use WOULD com verbos de estado (be, have, live, know) — 'I would live in NY' NÃO é hábito, é hipotético (segunda condicional). Para 'eu morava em NY', use USED TO.

examples.en: I used to live in NYC. ✓  (state — only used to works)
I would walk to Central Park every morning. ✓  (action — would works)
examples.pt: Eu caminhava para o Central Park toda manhã.

examples.en: I would live in NYC. ✗ (changes meaning to 'if given the chance')
examples.pt: Cuidado — troca o sentido para hipotético!

exercise.question: 'I ___ own a bookstore in New York.' Which is natural?
exercise.explanation: OWN is a state verb — USED TO only. 'Would own' would be hypothetical.
exercise.hint_pt_br: OWN é estado, só USED TO. WOULD OWN mudaria para hipotético.

exercise.question: Which TWO are correct for 'nós tomávamos café todo domingo'?
options: We used to have coffee every Sunday
We would have coffee every Sunday
exercise.explanation: HAVE COFFEE is a repeated action — both USED TO and WOULD work. Either is natural.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ação repetida: pode USED TO ou WOULD. Os dois funcionam.

turns.en: Did you use to read books there, or just sell them?
turns.pt: Você lia livros lá, ou só vendia?

turns.en: I would read one every day. Sometimes two. I used to be obsessive.
turns.pt: Eu lia um por dia. Às vezes dois. Eu era obsessivo.

turns.en: Now I teach. But I still read. Just slower.
turns.pt: Agora eu ensino. Mas ainda leio. Só mais devagar.

exercise.question: Complete: 'When I was a kid, I ___ ___ play in the rain every summer.'
exercise.explanation: Past habit, repeated action — USED TO works perfectly. WOULD would also work here.
exercise.hint_pt_br: USED TO ou WOULD funciona; USED TO é mais comum em fala espontânea.

pairs: I used to live in NY
exercise.explanation: Four examples. If it's a STATE (be, have, live, know), only USED TO. If it's an ACTION, either.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Estado → só USED TO. Ação → os dois.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Mr. Kumar's NYC Years". USED TO and WOULD for past habits and repeated actions. Mr. Kumar tells how he ran a bookstore in New York — and what he used to do every rainy Monday. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Mr. Kumar's NYC Years". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Mr. Kumar's NYC Years'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Mr. Kumar's NYC Years".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: Oxford Learner's Dictionary
sources.pt_hint: Dicionário Oxford com exemplos e áudio.

sources.label: BBC 6 Minute English
sources.pt_hint: Podcast de 6 minutos com transcrição — nível B1.
